proved: Export is deterministic at a fixed timestamp and contains only the normalized project manifest plus the exact Yjs document update.
The envelope and Yjs update have independent SHA-256 checksums; unknown envelope or manifest fields, future schemas, malformed base64, mismatched lengths, and tampering fail closed.
The live document GUID and shared project/schema metadata must match the manifest at export and import.
Import preserves stable project/document identity and every reserved shared collection while removing archived state and resetting transport to local.
Existing project-ID or document-ID collisions are rejected without overwriting or silently forking.
Different orphaned IndexedDB state under the imported project key is rejected rather than merged silently.
A decoded archive persists and reopens from fake IndexedDB without a network provider or model engine.
The product exposes import with no project open, gates export on a synchronized live document, bounds file size, and renders accessible status/error feedback.

The complete workspace suite, complete frontend suite, production frontend build, 42-check structural audit, and packaged Tauri/NSIS build pass under the bounded operation watchdog; the log confirms Compiling app v0.1.10.
notProved: The decisive two-clean-install test: export from one packaged installation, transfer the archive, disconnect networking, and import on a second packaged installation.
Crash or power-loss durability during archive persistence and manifest registration.
Large-project performance near the 25 MB Yjs update limit or 36 MB archive file limit.
Packaged save/open dialog interaction, screen-reader announcements, narrow-layout focus behavior, or dark-theme visual review.
Archive encryption, attachment-file inclusion, merge/fork workflows for an already-present project, or Drive/WebSocket transport restoration.
A signed updater release containing this development checkpoint.
